source_pr.title: planner: fix wrong column offsets when processing dynamic pruning for IndexJoin
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #24631 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary: planner: fix wrong column offsets when processing dynamic pruning for IndexJoin

### What is changed and how it works?
Before this PR, when processing dynamic pruning for `IndexJoin` in `prunePartitionForInnerExecutor`, `indexJoinLookUpContent.keyCols` is used directly, but this is wrong.
`indexJoinLookUpContent.keyCols` is offsets of these columns after column pruning instead of their actual offsets in the original table.
For example, for a table with columns `(a, b, c)` and a query `select b, c from ...`, `keyCols` for `b` and `c` is 0 and 1 since `a` is not read by this query, but the actual offsets of `b` and `c` in the original table is `1` and `2`.
When processing dynamic pruning, what we need is their actual offsets in the original table since we need to reconstruct a row and do partition pruning on it.
So I propagate the original column IDs to the place handling dynamic pruning and use them to recalculate the actual offsets of these columns.


More test cases will be added by https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/24497.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- planner: fix wrong column offsets when processing dynamic pruning for IndexJoin
